151.1: I was the youngest among my brothers,  A nd a youth in my father’s house.
151.2: I used to feed my father’s flock,  A nd I found a lion and a wolf,  A nd slew them and tore them.
151.3: My hands made an organ,  A nd my fingers fashioned a harp.
151.4: Who will show me my Lord? He, my Lord, has become my God.
151.5: He sent His messenger and took me away from my father’s flock,  A nd anointed me with the oil of anointing.
151.6: My brothers, the fair and the tall,  I n them the Lord had no pleasure.
151.7: And I went forth to meet the Philistine,  A nd he cursed me by his idols.
151.8: But I drew his sword and cut off his head,  A nd took away the reproach from the sons of Israel.

152.1: With a loud voice glorify God; In the assembly of many proclaim His glory.
152.2: Amid the multitude of the upright glorify His praise; And speak of His glory with the righteous.
152.3: Join your soul to the good and to the perfect,  T o glorify the Most High.
152.4: Gather yourselves together to make known His strength; And do not be slow in showing forth His deliverance and His glory to all babies.
152.5: That the honor of the Lord may be known, wisdom has been given; And to tell of His works it has been made known to men:
152.6: To make known to babies His strength,  A nd to make them that lack understanding  or heart  to comprehend His glory;
152.7: Who are far from His entrances and distant from His gates:
152.8: Because the Lord of Jacob is exalted,  A nd His glory is on all His works.
152.9: And a man who glorifies the Most High, in him will He take pleasure; As in one who offers fine meal,  A nd as in one who offers male goats and calves;
152.10: And as in one who makes fat the altar with a multitude of burnt-offerings; And as the smell of incense from the hands of the just.
152.11: From your upright gates will be heard His voice,  A nd from the voice of the upright admonition.
152.12: And in their eating will be satisfying in truth,  A nd in their drinking, when they share together.
152.13: Their dwelling is in the law of the Most High,  A nd their speech is to make known His strength.
152.14: How far from the wicked is speech of Him,  A nd from all transgressors to know Him!
152.15: Behold, the eye of the Lord takes pity on the good,  A nd to them that glorify Him will He multiply mercy,  A nd from the time of evil will He deliver their soul.
152.16: Blessed is the Lord, who has delivered the wretched from the hand of the wicked; Who raises up a horn out of Jacob and a judge of the nations out of Israel;
152.17: That He may prolong His dwelling in Zion,  A nd may adorn our age in Jerusalem.

153.1: O Lord, I have cried to You; Listen to me.
153.2: I have lifted up my hands to Your holy dwelling-place; Incline Your ear to me.
153.3: And grant me my request; Do not withhold my prayer from me.
153.4: Build up my soul, and do not destroy it; And do not lay it bare before the wicked.
153.5: Them that repay evil things—turn them away from me, O judge of truth.
153.6: O Lord, do not judge me according to my sins,  B ecause no flesh is innocent before You.
153.7: Make plain to me, O Lord, Your law,  A nd teach me Your judgments;
153.8: And many will hear of Your works,  A nd the nations will praise Your honor.
153.9: Remember me and do not forget me; And do not lead me into things that are too hard for me.
153.10: Make the sins of my youth pass from me,  A nd my discipline—do not let them remember it against me.
153.11: Cleanse me, O Lord, from the evil leprosy,  A nd let it no longer come to me.
153.12: Dry up its roots from me,  A nd do not let its leaves sprout within me.
153.13: Great are You, O Lord; Therefore, my request will be fulfilled from before You.
153.14: To whom will I complain that he may give to me? And what can the strength of men add to me?
153.15: From before You, O Lord, is my confidence; I cried to the Lord and He heard me, and healed the breaking of my heart.
153.16: I slumbered and slept; I dreamed and was helped, and the Lord sustained me.
153.17: They severely pained my heart; I will return thanks because the Lord delivered me.
153.18: Now I will rejoice in their shame; I have hoped in You, and I will not be ashamed.
153.19: Give You honor for all time— even forever and ever!
153.20: Deliver Israel, Your chosen one,  A nd them of the house of Jacob Your proved one.

154.1: O God, O God, come to my aid; Help me and save me; Deliver my soul from the slayer.
154.2: Will I go down to Sheol by the mouth of the lion? Or will the wolf confound me?
154.3: Was it not enough for them that they lay in wait for my father’s flock,  A nd tore in pieces a sheep of my father’s drove,  B ut they were also wishing to destroy my soul?
154.4: Have pity, O Lord, and save Your holy one from destruction; That he may rehearse Your glories in all his times,  A nd may praise Your great Name:
154.5: When You have delivered him from the hands of the destroying lion and of the ravening wolf,  A nd when You have rescued my captivity from the hands of the wild beasts.
154.6: Quickly, O my Lord, send a deliverer from before You,  A nd draw me out of the gaping pit,  W hich imprisons me in its depths.

155.1: Praise the Lord, all you nations; Glorify Him, and bless His Name—
155.2: Who rescued the soul of His chosen one from the hands of death,  A nd delivered His holy one from destruction,
155.3: And saved me from the nets of Sheol,  A nd my soul from the pit that cannot be fathomed.
155.4: Because, before my deliverance could go forth from before Him,  I was well nearly torn in two pieces by two wild beasts.
155.5: But He sent His messenger,  A nd shut up from me the gaping mouths,  A nd rescued my life from destruction.
155.6: My soul will glorify Him and exalt Him,  B ecause of all His kindnesses which He has done and will do to me.
